successCriteria.description: src/modules/leave/leave.model.ts exists and exports LeaveType, LeaveRequestStatus, and LeaveRequest with fields id, employeeId, leaveType, startDate, endDate, status, approverEmployeeId, and createdAt.
src/modules/leave/leave.repository.ts exists and exports a LeaveRepository interface that imports LeaveRequest from ./leave.model and defines create, findById, and findByEmployeeId methods.
src/modules/leave/postgres-leave.repository.ts exists and exports PostgresLeaveRepository implementing LeaveRepository using PostgreSQL via pg.Pool.
A PostgreSQL migration exists that creates the leave_requests table with the specified columns, constraints, and indexes for employee_id and status.
Vitest repository tests verify create persistence, findById retrieval, and findByEmployeeId listing behavior using LeaveRequest types defined in leave.model.ts.

constraints: Follow the repository pattern: all database access for leave persistence must go through the LeaveRepository interface and its implementation.
LeaveRepository and PostgresLeaveRepository must operate on LeaveRequest types imported from src/modules/leave/leave.model.ts.
Implement LeaveType as ANNUAL | SICK | EMERGENCY and LeaveRequestStatus as PENDING | APPROVED | REJECTED.
PostgresLeaveRepository is the only LeaveRepository implementation in this phase and must use pg.Pool.
The leave_requests schema must match the provided SQL definition, including constraints and indexes.
All async repository operations must catch and handle errors; no unhandled promise rejections.
All state-changing operations must write an audit record in accordance with platform standards.
outOfScope: Fastify API endpoints for leave management (Phase 4)
RBAC enforcement logic beyond repository-layer scaffolding
Service-layer business workflows not explicitly described in this phase

ambiguities.description: The requested test file path is tests/unit/modules/leave/leave.repository.spec.ts, while the architecture notes define src/modules/leave/postgres-leave.repository.test.ts.
The intent says leave.model.ts should define the canonical LeaveRequest entity shape and DTOs used by persistence, but only the LeaveRequest interface is explicitly specified.
options: Define only LeaveType, LeaveRequestStatus, and LeaveRequest
Define additional persistence DTO types alongside LeaveRequest
